title: Headset
abstract: A headset includes two earcups that overlie and seal around the wearers ears, The headset includes a; system: for suspension of the earcups on a helmet, as well as a speaker enclosure that provides increased structural strength without taking up additional space on the board.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An earcup suspension assembly configured for use with a helmet, the earcup suspension assembly comprising: a helmet connector configured for fixed connection to the helmet thereby to support the assembly on the helmet; a main arm extending from the helmet connector; a first joint between the helmet connector and the main arm, the first joint supporting the main arm on the helmet connector for pivotal movement relative to the helmet connector about a first axis; an earcup bracket extending from the main arm, the earcup bracket being configured to connect with and support an earcup; and a second joint between the main arm and the earcup bracket, the second joint supporting the earcup bracket on the support arm for pivotal movement relative to the support arm about a second axis. 2. An earcup suspension assembly as set forth in claim 1 further comprising a third joint between the main arm and the helmet connector and at the location of the first joint, enabling flipping movement of the main arm about the third joint in a direction toward and away from the ear of a wearer of the helmet; and the assembly comprising a biasing member acting between the main arm and the helmet connector thereby to bias the main arm about the third joint in a direction toward the ear of a wearer of the helmet. 3. An earcup suspension assembly as set forth in claim 2 further comprising a fourth joint between the main arm and the earcup connector and at the location of the second joint, the fourth joint enabling flipping movement of the earcup connector about the fourth joint in a direction toward and away from the ear of a wearer of the helmet. 4. An earcup suspension assembly as set forth in claim 1 wherein the earcup connector is shaped generally as a C-shaped band extending around and damping onto opposite of the earcup. 5. An earcup suspension assembly as set forth in claim 1 and further comprising an earcup including a speaker, the speaker having a central portion: the speaker having two mounting portions extending from the central portion, each mounting portion having a fastener opening for receiving a fastener to mount the speaker on the board, the two mounting portions being mirror images of each other; each mounting portion being asymmetrical and having first and second sections located on opposite sides of the fastener opening, the first section being substantially smaller in area than the second mounting section. 6. An earcup suspension assembly as set forth in claim 5 and further comprising an earcup including a battery compartment configured to accept either one of two different sizes of batteries to power electrical elements of the earcup. 7. An earcup; a printed circuit board in the earcup; and at least one speaker mounted on the board; the speaker having a central operative portion; the speaker having two mounting portions extending from tfie central portion, each mounting portion having a fastener opening for receiving a fastener to 

 mount the speaker on the board, the two mounting portions being mirror images of each other; each mounting portion being asymmetrical and having first and second sections located on opposite sides of the fastener opening, the first section being substantially smaller in area than the second mounting section. 8. An earcup as set forth in claim 7 wherein the two fastener openings are located on a centertine of the speaker, and the mourning portions extend from opposite ends of the central portion. 9. An earcup as set forth in claim 8 wherein the central portion of the speaker has a circular outer periphery, and each mounting portion has an outer edge surface that extends from the circular outer periphery of the central portion as a continuation of the outer periphery of the central portion; the outer edge surface includes a first segment that extends from the central portion outer periphery in a direction parallel to the centerline and just past the fastener opening. the outer edge surface includes a second segment that is formed as a circular arc extending from the first segment and around to the opposite edge of the fastener opening; and the outer edge surface includes a third segment that extends in a straight line from the second segment to the central portion outer periphery and that lies tangent to the circular outer periphery of the central portion. 10. An earcup as set forth in claim 7 including a battery compartment configured to accept either one of two different sizes of batteries to power electrical elements of the earcup.
